title: Rules of Engagement FAQ
description: Common expectations for participating in the blog community.

items.question: How should I engage with other members?
items.answer: Be respectful, professional, and constructive. Debate ideas directly without insulting, harassing, or targeting people.

items.question: What counts as a relevant comment or post?
items.answer: Keep contributions related to the blog post, discussion thread, or community topic. Move unrelated questions or side conversations to a more appropriate thread.

items.question: How should feedback be shared?
items.answer: Share feedback with enough context to be useful. Explain what you observed, why it matters, and, where possible, suggest a practical improvement.

items.question: Are promotions or repeated posts allowed?
items.answer: Spam, repeated posts, unsolicited promotions, and irrelevant links are not allowed. Only share links when they directly support the discussion.

items.question: Can I share personal or private information?
items.answer: Do not post private, confidential, or sensitive information about yourself, other members, clients, employers, or third parties.

items.question: Should I credit sources?
items.answer: Credit original authors, tools, articles, data, and media where relevant. Do not present someone else's work as your own.

items.question: What language is not acceptable?
items.answer: Discriminatory, hateful, threatening, or exclusionary language is not acceptable. Keep the blog welcoming for people from different backgrounds and experience levels.

items.question: What should I do if I see a problem?
items.answer: Report content that appears abusive, unsafe, misleading, or against these rules. Avoid escalating conflicts in the thread.

items.question: How are the rules enforced?
items.answer: Moderators may edit, hide, remove, or restrict content and accounts when needed to protect the quality and safety of the blog.
